parts.title: The Festival
parts.text: Every year, the small village held a **tradition** that filled the streets with music and dancing. This year, a great storm had swept through and left everyone feeling low. The mayor knew she had to act fast to **revive** the community's excitement before festival day arrived.

parts.title: The Preparation
parts.text: She walked through the village with clear **intent**: she was going to make this the best festival yet. Her **approach** was simple — talk to every neighbor and ask them to help decorate. The decorations were **vibrant**, with bold colors bursting from every window and doorway. Children painted a cheerful **pattern** of suns and waves along the town wall.

parts.title: The Celebration
parts.text: When festival day came, the crowd cheered with great **glory** as the parade began. A young girl who had always been quiet and **meek** stood up and recited a poem with a clear, loud voice. She had used her **wit** to write something funny and touching. The mayor gave a **prompt** nod to start the fireworks right on time. The storm had arrived with **fury**, but the festival proved the village could not be broken. Everyone would **approve** of how the day turned out.

parts.title: The Memory
parts.text: That night, the whole village sat under the stars and shared stories. The mayor smiled and said the best festivals are not the ones with the most money — they are the ones where everyone comes together. The children promised to help decorate again next year, and the tradition grew even stronger.

definitions.meaning1: Extreme anger or rage. Example: The giant flew into a fury when he found his gold had been stolen.
definitions.meaning2: Wild and violent force. Example: The fury of the storm knocked down several trees.
definitions.studentChallenge: She felt the ___ rising inside her when she saw the broken window.

definitions.meaning1: A plan or purpose. Example: It was my intent to clean my room, but I fell asleep instead.
definitions.meaning2: Paying very close attention. Example: The cat watched the mouse with intent eyes.
definitions.studentChallenge: Her ___ was to finish the book before the weekend.

definitions.meaning1: A repeated design or decoration. Example: The wallpaper has a beautiful flower pattern.
definitions.meaning2: A regular way something happens. Example: The teacher noticed a pattern of kindness among the students.
definitions.studentChallenge: The artist used a repeating ___ of stars and moons on the fabric.

definitions.meaning1: Bright, strong, and full of energy. Example: The flowers in the garden were vibrant, with bright yellows and deep reds.
definitions.studentChallenge: The mural on the school wall was ___ and full of life.

definitions.meaning1: The ability to say things that are clever and funny. Example: Her quick wit made everyone laugh during the long car ride.
definitions.studentChallenge: It takes real ___ to write jokes that are both clever and kind.

definitions.meaning1: A way of dealing with something. Example: The team had to decide on the best approach for solving the problem.
definitions.meaning2: To move closer to something. Example: She began to approach the podium slowly, nervous about her speech.
definitions.studentChallenge: We need a new ___ to this problem because the old way isn't working.

definitions.meaning1: To agree with or accept something. Example: The principal was happy to approve the new lunch menu.
definitions.meaning2: To have a good opinion of something. Example: Her parents did not approve of staying up past midnight.
definitions.studentChallenge: The teacher had to ___ the field trip plan before we could go.

definitions.meaning1: Great fame, honor, or praise. Example: The old painting still showed the glory of the ancient kingdom.
definitions.meaning2: Beauty or magnificence. Example: The glory of the sunset took everyone's breath away.
definitions.studentChallenge: The crowd cheered as the team celebrated their ___ after winning the championship.

definitions.meaning1: Quiet, gentle, and not wanting to argue. Example: The meek child gave a soft nod and stepped aside.
definitions.studentChallenge: The shy lamb was quiet and ___ around the other animals.

definitions.meaning1: A custom or belief passed down from generation to generation. Example: Lighting candles on birthdays is a tradition many families share.
definitions.studentChallenge: The old custom had become a beloved family ___ over the years.

definitions.meaning1: Done quickly and without delay; on time. Example: A prompt reply showed she had been paying attention.
definitions.meaning2: To cause someone to do something. Example: The teacher's reminder prompted students to finish the assignment.
definitions.studentChallenge: The bus driver is always ___ — he arrives at exactly 7:15 every morning.

definitions.meaning1: To bring back to life, health, or strength. Example: The cool water helped revive the wilting plants after the hot day.
definitions.meaning2: To bring back into use or popularity. Example: The coach gave a speech to revive the team's spirit.
definitions.studentChallenge: A cold glass of water was enough to ___ her energy after the long run.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The artist used a repeating _______ of stars and moons on the fabric she was designing.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The teacher noticed a _______ of kindness in the way the students helped one another every day.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The crowd cheered as the team celebrated their _______ after winning the championship.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The old painting still showed the _______ of the ancient kingdom in all its golden detail.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The teacher's _______ reminder helped students finish the assignment right on time.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: A _______ reply to the question showed she had been paying close attention all along.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The storm hit with great _______, knocking down trees across the entire neighborhood.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: In the story, the giant flew into a _______ when he found that his gold had been stolen.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The shy lamb was quiet and _______ around the other animals in the barnyard.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: He gave a _______ nod and stepped aside to let the others go first without arguing.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Her quick _______ humor made everyone laugh during the long, boring car ride.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: It takes real _______ to write jokes that are both clever and funny at the same time.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The flowers in the garden were _______, with bright yellows and deep reds bursting everywhere.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The mural painted on the school wall was _______ and full of energy and life.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The team had to decide on the best _______ for solving the difficult math problem.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: She began to _______ the podium slowly, nervous about giving her very first speech.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: His _______ was to finish the entire book before the weekend started.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: She stared at the puzzle with a look of deep _______, determined to solve it no matter what.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The parents did not _______ of staying up past midnight on school nights.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The principal was happy to _______ the new lunch menu after tasting the food herself.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The old custom had become a beloved family _______ passed down for generations.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Lighting candles on birthdays is a _______ that many families around the world share.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The cool water helped _______ the wilting plants after the long, hot summer day.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The coach gave a powerful speech to _______ the team's spirit before the championship game.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage:
Every year, the small village held a _(1)_ that filled the streets with music and dancing. It had been celebrated for hundreds of years, and no one wanted to let it fade away. This year, though, a great storm had swept through and left everyone feeling low. The mayor knew she had to act fast to _(2)_ the community's excitement before festival day arrived.

She walked through the village with clear _(3)_: she was going to make this the best festival yet. Her _(4)_ was simple — talk to every neighbor and ask them to help decorate. The decorations were _(5)_, with bold colors bursting from every window and doorway. Children painted a cheerful _(6)_ of suns and waves along the town wall.

When festival day came, the crowd cheered with great _(7)_ as the parade began. A young girl who had always been quiet and _(8)_ stood up and recited a poem with a clear, loud voice. She had used her _(9)_ to write something both funny and touching. The mayor gave a _(10)_ nod to start the fireworks right on time. The storm had arrived with _(11)_, but the festival proved the village could not be broken. Everyone would _(12)_ of how the day turned out.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
